openmeter/app/stripe/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing the appstripe.Adapter interface; owns all DB reads and writes for Stripe app config (AppStripe entity), Stripe-customer mappings (AppStripeCustomer entity), and secret retrieval. All mutations go through entutils.TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue so ctx-bound transactions are honored.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every mutation
patterns.description: Every method that writes to the DB must be wrapped with entutils.TransactingRepo or TransactingRepoWithNoValue(ctx, a, func(ctx, repo *adapter) ...). Never call repo.db directly outside that wrapper.
patterns.example: entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, repo *adapter) (T, error) { ... repo.db.AppStripe.Create()... })

patterns.name: WithTx / Self / Tx triad
patterns.description: adapter implements TxCreator via Tx(), rebinds to an existing tx via WithTx(), and returns itself via Self(). These three methods are the contract that entutils.TransactingRepo relies on — they must stay consistent.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txClient := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txClient.Client(), ...} }

patterns.name: Input.Validate() before any DB call
patterns.description: Every exported adapter method calls input.Validate() first and wraps validation failures in models.NewGenericValidationError.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return ..., models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("error ...: %w", err)) }

patterns.name: entdb.IsNotFound / IsConstraintError error mapping
patterns.description: Map entdb.IsNotFound to app.NewAppNotFoundError or app.NewAppCustomerPreConditionError; map entdb.IsConstraintError to models.NewGenericConflictError.
patterns.example: if entdb.IsNotFound(err) { return ..., app.NewAppNotFoundError(input.AppID) }

patterns.name: Config.Validate() in New()
patterns.description: The constructor calls config.Validate() and returns an error on failure. All required fields (Client, AppService, CustomerService, SecretService, Logger) must be present.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (appstripe.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, ... } }

key_file_guides.role: Adapter struct definition, Config, New constructor, Tx/WithTx/Self transaction helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithTx must copy all factory fields (stripeClientFactory, stripeAppClientFactory) when rebinding; missing a field silently breaks calls inside the transaction.

key_file_guides.role: GetStripeCustomerData, UpsertStripeCustomerData, DeleteStripeCustomerData, createStripeCustomer — all Stripe-customer mapping operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpsertStripeCustomerData calls app.EnsureCustomer inside the transaction before the actual upsert; skipping EnsureCustomer breaks the app-customer relationship FK.

key_file_guides.role: CreateStripeApp, UpdateAPIKey, GetStripeAppData, DeleteStripeAppData, GetWebhookSecret, SetCustomerDefaultPaymentMethod, CreateCheckoutSession.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetWebhookSecret intentionally omits namespace filter (webhook payload is signed, namespace is unknown before validation); do not add a namespace predicate there.

anti_patterns: Calling repo.db methods directly without entutils.TransactingRepo — bypasses ctx-bound transaction.
Returning raw ent errors to callers instead of mapping them to app.NewAppNotFoundError / models.NewGenericConflictError.
Implementing new business logic here — this is a pure persistence layer; all orchestration belongs in appservice.

decisions.decision: Adapter holds both StripeClientFactory and StripeAppClientFactory rather than creating clients inline.
decisions.rationale: Allows tests to inject a mock factory without hitting the real Stripe API.

decisions.decision: WithTx creates a new adapter value (not pointer mutation) so the original adapter is not affected by transaction rebinding.
decisions.rationale: Prevents accidental cross-request transaction leakage when multiple goroutines hold the same adapter pointer.

code_examples.scenario: Writing a new adapter method that upserts a row inside the caller's transaction

openmeter/app/stripe/client.purpose: Pure Stripe API client layer with two distinct client types: StripeClient (pre-install, for OAuth/webhook setup) and StripeAppClient (post-install, for all app operations including invoices, customers, checkout). No business logic; all error translation to OpenMeter domain errors happens here.

patterns.name: Dual client hierarchy
patterns.description: StripeClient (client.go) is for unauthenticated/pre-install calls (GetAccount, SetupWebhook). StripeAppClient (appclient.go) is for installed-app calls (invoices, customers, portal). Never use StripeClient for post-install operations.
patterns.example: stripeClient, _ := NewStripeClient(StripeClientConfig{...}); stripeAppClient, _ := NewStripeAppClient(StripeAppClientConfig{...})

patterns.name: providerError translation
patterns.description: Every method that calls the Stripe API must pass raw errors through providerError(). 400 maps to models.NewGenericValidationError, 401 maps to app.NewAppProviderAuthenticationError (and also updates app status to Unauthorized for stripeAppClient), others map to app.NewAppProviderError.
patterns.example: if err != nil { return ..., c.providerError(err) }

patterns.name: OpenMeter metadata injection on Stripe objects
patterns.description: Every Stripe object created by OpenMeter (customer, invoice, checkout session, webhook, setup intent) must have StripeMetadataNamespace, StripeMetadataAppID, and StripeMetadataCustomerID injected into its Metadata map.
patterns.example: Metadata: map[string]string{StripeMetadataNamespace: input.AppID.Namespace, StripeMetadataAppID: input.AppID.ID, ...}

patterns.name: Input.Validate() on every public method
patterns.description: All Input structs implement Validate() error; call it at the start of every method before touching the Stripe API.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return ..., models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }

patterns.name: Idempotency keys on write operations
patterns.description: Stripe write operations (CreateInvoice) use params.SetIdempotencyKey with a deterministic key derived from the OpenMeter entity ID to prevent duplicate creation on retries.
patterns.example: params.SetIdempotencyKey(fmt.Sprintf("invoice-create-%s", input.InvoiceID))

patterns.name: leveledLogger bridge
patterns.description: All stripe.Backend instances must be constructed with the leveledLogger bridge (logger.go) so Stripe SDK log output routes through slog. Never use the default Stripe logger.
patterns.example: stripe.GetBackendWithConfig(stripe.APIBackend, &stripe.BackendConfig{LeveledLogger: leveledLogger{logger: config.Logger}})

key_file_guides.role: StripeAppClient interface + concrete stripeAppClient implementation; also defines all webhook event type constants and reserved metadata keys.
key_file_guides.watch_for: providerError in stripeAppClient calls UpdateAppStatus to AppStatusUnauthorized on 401 — it uses context.Background() for the app status update, which is intentional but violates the general no-context.Background() rule.

key_file_guides.role: StripeClient interface + concrete stripeClient; also Currency/FromStripeCurrency helpers and IsAPIKeyLiveMode.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Currency conversion: OpenMeter uses uppercase ISO codes (currencyx.Code), Stripe uses lowercase. Always use Currency() / FromStripeCurrency() helpers, never manual string conversion.

key_file_guides.role: CreateInvoice, UpdateInvoice, DeleteInvoice, FinalizeInvoice, GetInvoice with all their input/output types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AutoAdvance is always set to false on creation; Stripe advancement is driven by FinalizeInvoice. CollectionMethod determines whether DaysUntilDue is required.

key_file_guides.role: AddInvoiceLines, UpdateInvoiceLines, RemoveInvoiceLines. Adding lines requires a post-create list call to get line IDs because Stripe CreateInvoiceItem does not return the line ID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AddInvoiceLines always calls ListInvoiceLineItems after creation to resolve LineID — this is by design, not a bug.

key_file_guides.role: Typed Stripe-specific errors (StripeCustomerNotFoundError, StripePaymentMethodNotFoundError, StripeInvoiceCustomerTaxLocationInvalidError) with Is* helpers for errors.As checks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: These errors implement models.GenericError but are NOT the same as generic not-found errors; callers must use IsStripeCustomerNotFoundError etc. rather than models.IsNotFoundError.

anti_patterns: Calling stripe API methods without routing the error through providerError() — misses 401 app-status update and domain error mapping.
Creating Stripe objects (customers, invoices) without injecting StripeMetadataNamespace / StripeMetadataAppID in Metadata.
Using raw string currency codes instead of Currency() / FromStripeCurrency() helpers.
Adding business logic here — this package is a thin API wrapper; all orchestration belongs in appservice.

decisions.decision: Two separate client interfaces (StripeClient vs StripeAppClient) rather than one unified client.
decisions.rationale: Pre-install calls (webhook setup, account retrieval) do not have an installed app context; merging them would require optional fields or nil checks throughout.

decisions.decision: AddInvoiceLines does a separate ListInvoiceLineItems call to resolve Stripe line IDs.
decisions.rationale: Stripe's InvoiceItems.New returns the invoice item but not its line item ID; the line ID is required for subsequent update/delete operations.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new stripeAppClient method that calls the Stripe API

openmeter/app/stripe/httpdriver.purpose: HTTP handler layer for all Stripe-specific API endpoints: webhook ingestion, API key rotation, checkout session creation, customer Stripe data CRUD, and portal session creation. Uses the httptransport.Handler / HandlerWithArgs pattern; delegates all business logic to appstripe.Service and billing.Service.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs
patterns.description: Every handler function returns httptransport.Handler[Req, Resp] or httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Req, Resp, Params]. The three arguments are: decoder (context + http.Request → Req), operation (context + Req → Resp), and encoder (http status + Resp). Never implement ServeHTTP directly.
patterns.example: return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoderFn, operationFn, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[Resp](http.StatusOK), httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("..."))...)

patterns.name: resolveNamespace from context
patterns.description: All decoders call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) to extract the namespace. Never read the namespace from the URL or request body directly.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return Req{}, err }

patterns.name: Deleted customer guard before use
patterns.description: After resolving a customer from the service, always check cus.IsDeleted() and return models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError if true.
patterns.example: if cus != nil && cus.IsDeleted() { return Req{}, models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError(fmt.Errorf("customer is deleted ...")) }

patterns.name: Webhook namespace from signed secret, not request
patterns.description: The webhook handler (AppStripeWebhook) does NOT call resolveNamespace. It recovers the namespace from secret.SecretID.Namespace after validating the Stripe-Signature header. Never inject a namespace from the URL for webhooks.
patterns.example: secret, err := h.service.GetWebhookSecret(ctx, ...); event, err := webhook.ConstructEventWithTolerance(..., secret.Value, ...); appID := app.AppID{Namespace: secret.SecretID.Namespace, ID: params.AppID}

patterns.name: Handler interface composed from sub-interfaces
patterns.description: Handler interface in handler.go embeds AppStripeHandler; the concrete handler struct implements all methods. New exported types for each request/response use type aliases (`type X = appstripe.Y`) to avoid unnecessary wrapper types.
patterns.example:
type Handler interface { AppStripeHandler }
type AppStripeHandler interface { AppStripeWebhook() ...; UpdateStripeAPIKey() ... }

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface definition, concrete handler struct, New() constructor, resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: handler depends on billingService AND customerService in addition to the stripe service — new handlers that need billing or customer resolution can access them directly.

key_file_guides.role: AppStripeWebhook handler — validates Stripe-Signature, routes 12+ event types to service calls.
key_file_guides.watch_for: setup_intent.succeeded events must validate StripeMetadataAppID and StripeMetadataNamespace in payment intent metadata before acting. Events missing the app metadata are silently ignored (non-OpenMeter-initiated payments).

key_file_guides.role: CreateAppStripeCheckoutSession — handles three customer resolution paths: create-by-inline-data, resolve-by-ID, resolve-by-key.
key_file_guides.watch_for: App ID resolution falls back to billing profile lookup (h.billingService.ResolveStripeAppIDFromBillingProfile) when body.AppId is nil — do not remove this fallback.

key_file_guides.role: Pure conversion functions between domain and API types (toAPIStripePortalSession, fromAPIAppStripeCustomerDataBase).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep mapping functions pure (no service calls); all enrichment happens in the operation closure.

anti_patterns: Calling service methods in the decoder function — decoders must only parse/validate the request shape.
Reading namespace from URL path or body instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx).
Adding business logic to handlers — all domain decisions belong in appstripe.Service.

decisions.decision: Webhook handler recovers namespace from the signed secret rather than a URL segment.
decisions.rationale: Stripe webhook calls include the app ID in the URL but not the namespace; the only secure source of namespace is the stored webhook secret whose ID carries the namespace.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new Stripe HTTP handler that resolves a customer and delegates to the service

openmeter/app/stripe/service.purpose: Business logic service implementing appstripe.Service and app.AppFactory; orchestrates Stripe app install/uninstall lifecycle, webhook event handling, invoice state transitions, and checkout session creation. Delegates persistence to appstripe.Adapter and Stripe API calls to the client factories injected through the adapter.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wrapping every service method
patterns.description: Service methods that call the adapter must wrap in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx) ...) or transaction.RunWithNoValue. This ensures a single transaction spans the operation.
patterns.example: func (s *Service) GetWebhookSecret(...) (..., error) { return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (..., error) { return s.adapter.GetWebhookSecret(ctx, input) }) }

patterns.name: Marketplace registration in New()
patterns.description: New() must call config.AppService.RegisterMarketplaceListing with the factory (Service itself) and the stripe marketplace listing. This self-registration is what makes the Stripe app type installable.
patterns.example: config.AppService.RegisterMarketplaceListing(app.RegistryItem{Listing: appstripe.StripeMarketplaceListing, Factory: service})

patterns.name: Config.Validate() then New()
patterns.description: All constructor dependencies are validated via Config.Validate() before constructing the Service. Return error from New() if validation fails — never panic.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Publisher.Publish after adapter mutation
patterns.description: Side-effect events (checkout session created, payment setup succeeded) are published via s.publisher.Publish() inside the transaction.Run closure, after the adapter write succeeds.
patterns.example: event := appstripe.NewAppCheckoutSessionEvent(ctx, ...); if err := s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event); err != nil { return ..., fmt.Errorf("failed to publish event: %w", err) }

patterns.name: HandleInvoiceStateTransition idempotency guards
patterns.description: Before triggering a billing state transition, check if the invoice is already in TargetStatuses (skip) or matches IgnoreInvoiceInStatus (skip). Then optionally re-fetch from Stripe via GetStripeInvoice to validate the event is not stale.
patterns.example:
if slices.Contains(input.TargetStatuses, invoice.Status) { return nil } // already there
if invoice.Status.Matches(input.IgnoreInvoiceInStatus...) { return nil } // terminal

patterns.name: newApp combines AppBase with AppData
patterns.description: After any adapter call that returns AppBase + AppData, call s.newApp(appBase, stripeApp) to produce the concrete appstripe.App struct with all injected service references.
patterns.example: app, err := s.newApp(stripeApp.AppBase, stripeApp.AppData)

key_file_guides.role: Service struct, Config, New() constructor with marketplace self-registration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DisableWebhookRegistration flag (for dev/test) is respected in factory.go; do not reference it in other files.

InstallAppWithAPIKey creates secrets and the Stripe webhook before the DB record — a partial failure can leave orphaned secrets or webhooks; the TODO comment acknowledges this and no rollback exists yet.
getInvoiceByStripeID returns nil (not error) when the Stripe invoice is unknown to OpenMeter — callers must check for nil before proceeding.
patternWebhookURLGenerator requires a %s placeholder in the pattern; validated at construction time.

key_file_guides.role: Remaining appstripe.Service methods: GetWebhookSecret, UpdateAPIKey, CreateCheckoutSession, GetStripeAppData, GetStripeCustomerData, UpsertStripeCustomerData, DeleteStripeCustomerData, HandleSetupIntentSucceeded, CreatePortalSession.
key_file_guides.watch_for: generateMaskedSecretAPIKey is a local helper that must be kept in sync with the masking format displayed in the UI.

anti_patterns: Calling s.adapter methods directly without transaction.Run — bypasses transaction boundary.
Implementing InvoiceStateTransition without the idempotency guards (target-status check, ignore-status check) — causes duplicate billing state transitions.
Not calling RegisterMarketplaceListing in New() — makes Stripe app type invisible to the marketplace registry.

decisions.decision: Service self-registers into the app marketplace in New() rather than at wire time.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the registration co-located with the implementation; the app.Service.RegisterMarketplaceListing is idempotent so duplicate registrations are safe.

decisions.decision: InstallAppWithAPIKey generates the app ID before creating secrets/webhook to enable pre-creation secret labeling.
decisions.rationale: Secrets must be tagged with AppID for retrieval; the ID must exist before any secret or webhook is created, so it is generated early with ulid.Make().

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new service method that writes to the adapter and publishes an event
